channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: Advancing IBD Management: A Literature Review on the Role of Non-Invasive Blood-Based Biomarkers in Predicting and Assessing Pharmacodynamic Response to Treatment.
item.description: Clinical and translational science - Review - Reliable biomarkers that enable noninvasive, longitudinal assessment of disease activity and therapeutic response remain a major unmet need in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). While colonic biopsi...

item.title: Development and validation of a novel latex particle-enhanced turbidimetric immunoassay for fecal calprotectin.
item.description: Biotechnology letters - Fecal calprotectin (FCP) is a crucial non-invasive biomarker for diagnosing and monitoring inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the standard enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) is time-...

item.title: Phospholipid-chemoattractant pathways: new pharmacological targets for stratified treatment of immune-mediated diseases.
item.description: Biochemical pharmacology - Review - Precision Pharmacology in immune-mediated disorders remains constrained by the limited ability of static genomic and proteomic frameworks to resolve the dynamic signalling circuits that govern immu...

item.title: Urinary Calprotectin Is Not a Reliable Surrogate of Fecal Calprotectin Levels in Adults and Children with Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Fecal calprotectin (FCAL) is an established biomarker for monitoring intestinal inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease. However, fecal sampling can be socially unacceptable and inconvenient. Ur...

item.title: Neutrophil-Derived Reactive Oxygen Species and Bystander Tissue Damage in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Free radical biology & medicine - Review - Neutrophils (PMNs) are indispensable effectors of innate immunity whose oxidative and proteolytic capabilities permit rapid microbial containment at mucosal surfaces. Nowhere is this more functiona...

item.title: Identifying omic biomarkers for chronic inflammatory diseases associated with periodontitis using percolation on multi-disease gene co-expression networks.
item.description: Communications medicine - Chronic inflammatory diseases, such as ulcerative colitis (UC), Crohn's disease (CD), Alzheimer's disease (AD) and Parkinson's disease (PD) are clinically related to periodontitis. However, the com...

item.title: Integrated RNA-seq and scRNA-seq to explore the biological mechanisms of mitophagy-related genes in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: PloS one - Mitophagy's role in ulcerative colitis (UC) is not fully understood. This study explores mitophagy's impact on UC and aims to create a diagnostic model. The transcriptomic datasets of patients with...

item.title: Confounding of sex-biased gene expression in Crohn's disease recurrence.
Thymoquinone and caffeic acid phenethyl ester mitigate experimental ulcerative colitis: a biochemical and histopathological study.
item.description: Acta cirurgica brasileira - To establish an acetic acid-induced acute ulcerative colitis model in rats and to evaluate the clinical, biochemical, and histopathological effects of thymoquinone (TQ) and caffeic acid phenethyl e...

item.title: The role of claudins in the physiology and pathology of the digestive system.
item.description: Biochemical and biophysical research communications - Review - Claudins (CLDNs) are small transmembrane proteins that constitute a key component of tight junctions, playing a crucial role in maintaining epithelial barrier integrity and regulating paracellular ...

item.title: Beyond the Gut: Extra-Enteric Digestive Manifestations of Inflammatory Bowel Disease-A Personalized Medicine Perspective and Comprehensive Review.
item.description: Journal of personalized medicine - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)-including Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, and indeterminate colitis-is a chronic immune-mediated condition that primarily affects the intestinal mucosa but oft...

item.title: Multi-omics analysis identifies oxidative stress-related biomarkers and therapeutic targets linking periodontitis and ulcerative colitis via the oral-gut axis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Periodontitis (PD) and ulcerative colitis (UC) are two common chronic inflammatory diseases increasingly connected via the "oral-gut axis," yet their shared molecular mechanisms remain unclear. Oxi...
